papers.title_en: The Thinking Pixel: Recursive Sparse Reasoning in Multimodal Diffusion Latents
papers.authors: Yuwei Sun, Yuxuan Yao, Hui Li, Siyu Zhu
papers.evidence: 受自回归推理启发的扩散潜空间递归细化方法
papers.tldr: 针对扩散模型在复杂结构化推理（如文本遵循）方面的局限性，本文受人类认知启发，提出了一种集成在扩散模型中的递归稀疏专家混合（MoE）框架。该方法在联合注意力层中引入递归组件，通过稀疏选择神经模块在多个潜步中迭代优化视觉标记，在保持参数效率的同时增强了模型的推理能力，显著提升了在ImageNet、GenEval和DPG基准上的图像生成性能。
papers.abstract_en: Diffusion models have achieved success in high-fidelity data synthesis, yet their capacity for more complex, structured reasoning like text following tasks remains constrained. While advances in language models have leveraged strategies such as latent reasoning and recursion to enhance text understanding capabilities, extending these to multimodal text-to-image generation tasks is challenging due to the continuous and non-discrete nature of visual tokens. To tackle this problem, we draw inspiration from modular human cognition and propose a recursive, sparse mixture-of-experts framework integrated into conventional diffusion models. Our approach introduces a recursive component within joint attention layers that iteratively refines visual tokens over multiple latent steps while efficiently sharing parameters via sparse selection of neural modules. At each step, a gating network is devised to dynamically select specialized neural modules, conditioned on the current visual tokens, the diffusion timestep, and the conditioning information. Comprehensive evaluation on class-conditioned ImageNet image generation tasks and additional studies on the GenEval and DPG benchmark demonstrate the superiority of the proposed method in enhancing model image generation performance.
